title: DOH Workshop Protocol Part 1: Purification of High- Molecular Weight Genomic DNA from Gram-Negative Bacteria  (MagAttract HMW DNA)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1qfee","text":"This protocol enables extraction of high-molecular-weight DNA from gram-negative bacterial cultures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pellet 1.5 mL of the Gram negative bacterial cell culture by spinning at 5000 x g, °C, for 00:03:00 . When removing tube from centrifuge, be careful not to shake the tube too much which can dislodge the pellet.

Remove and discard supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Use a P200/P20 pipette if there is a small amount of supernatant remaining. The cell pellet can be stored at -30 °C to -15 °C or -90 °C to -60 °C for future use, or can be used immediately.
Resuspend the bacterial pellet in 180 µL Buffer ATL, add 20 µL Proteinase K and mix by flicking/stirring the tube. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA

Incubate for 00:30:00 on thermomixer at 56 °C shaking at 900rpm. Things to do while waiting: Ensure that Buffers MW1 and PE were prepared according to instructions on them (i.e. adding appropriate amount of Ethanol).
Add 4 µL RNase A to the sample, mix by pulse vortexing or by tapping the tube several times, and incubate for 00:02:00 at Room temperature . Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Set the thermomixer to 21 °C to give it time to cool for next steps.

DOH Workshop Protocol Part 1Fully mix MagAttract Suspension G. Vortex the MagAttract Suspension G vigorously until thoroughly mixed ∼ 00:00:10 . It is important that beads are fully mixed and re-mixed between every few samples. Shake well before use
Add 15 µL MagAttract Suspension G and 280 µL Buffer MB to the sample. Mix by pulse vortexing ( 00:00:01 - 00:00:02 quick presses x 3). Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm..
Place tubes onto magnetic rack and wait until beads pellet (∼ 00:01:00 ).
While leaving tubes on the magnet, carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the beads using a P1000. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Remove tube from magnetic rack and add 700 µL Buffer MW1 to the tube, flick the tube until bead pellet is unstuck from tube wall. MW1 Wash 1
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm.
Place tubes onto magnetic rack and wait until beads pellet (∼ 00:01:00 ).
While leaving tubes on the magnet, carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the beads using a P1000. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
For the second wash, remove tube from magnetic rack and add 700 µL Buffer MW1 to the tube, flick the tube until bead pellet is unstuck from tube wall. MW1 Wash 2
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm.
Place tubes onto magnetic rack and wait until beads pellet (∼ 00:01:00 ).
While leaving tubes on the magnet, carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the beads using a P1000. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Remove tube from magnetic rack and add 700 µL Buffer PE to the tube, flick the tube until bead pellet is unstuck from tube wall. PE Wash 1
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm..
Place tubes onto magnetic rack and wait until beads pellet (∼ 00:01:00 ).
While leaving tubes on the magnet, carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the beads using a P1000. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
For the second wash, remove tube from magnetic rack and add 700 µL Buffer PE to the tube, flick the tube until bead pellet is unstuck from tube wall. PE Wash 2
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm.
Place tubes onto magnetic rack and wait until beads pellet (∼ 00:01:00 ).

While leaving tubes on the magnet, carefully remove supernatant without disturbing the beads using a P1000. Then use a P200 to remove any traces of Buffer PE. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA

While keeping the tube on the magnetic rack, slowly pipette 700 µL distilled water by pipetting down the side of the tube opposite the pellet, without disturbing any of the beads/pellet. Leave for 00:00:30 - 00:01:00 and then remove the supernatant without disturbing any of the beads/pellet. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Repeat step 28. If necessary, use a P20 to remove any remaining distilled water. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA

Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 50 µL of nuclease free water. Resuspend the beads by slowly pipetting or stirring with the pipette tip. Be as gentle as possible while ensuring that pellet is resuspended. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA
Place the tube onto the thermomixer and incubate at t 21 °C for 00:03:00 at 1400 rpm.

Pellet beads on magnet and slowly pipette DNA eluate into a new lo-bind tube. Pipette gently, be careful not to shear DNA Save the tube with magnetic beads in case of incomplete DNA elution, so you can repeat.
creator: Bryan Wee
